{{Infobox ancient site |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped burial mound measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped burial mound measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped burial mound measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped burial mound measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped burial mound measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun |Group=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |Coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Dimensions=Total length 174 m (including moats) |Period=Late 6th century–early 7th century |Artifacts=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |HistoricSite=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |Burial=Horizontal stone chamber |Map2={{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is designated as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield shape, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. Artifacts unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun Period]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum
