revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun | part_of=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=Total length 174 m (including moats) |built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |burial goods=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |Historic Site=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |burial=Horizontal stone chamber |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|12=draft}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|12=draft}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} * [[Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|12=draft}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|12=draft}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|12=draft}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun | part_of=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=Total length 174 m (including moats) |built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |burial goods=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |Historic Site=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |burial=Horizontal stone chamber |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} * [[Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|12=draft}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|12=draft}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|12=draft}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} * [[Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|12=draft}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun | part_of=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=Total length 174 m (including moats) |built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |burial goods=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |Historic Site=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |burial=Horizontal stone chamber |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} * [[Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} * [[Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun | part_of=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=Total length 174 m (including moats) |built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |burial goods=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |Historic Site=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |burial=Horizontal stone chamber |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} * [[Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

revision.comment: 33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大堤権現塚古墳 up to rev 101132863
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=大堤権現塚古墳 |所属=大堤古墳群 |画像=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|大堤権現塚古墳]] |所在地=千葉県山武市松尾町大堤482 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長174m（周溝を含む） |築造年代=6世紀末～7世紀初頭 |出土品=頭椎大刀、圭頭大刀等 |史跡指定=1990年（平成2年）3月16日・千葉県指定 |埋葬施設=横穴式石室 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=大堤権現塚
古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=前方後円墳-南南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''大堤権現塚古墳'''（おおつつみごんげんづかこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武市]]松尾町大堤にある[[古墳]]。[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|千葉県の史跡]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|title=大堤権現塚古墳|publisher=千葉県|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|accessdate=2020-04-19}}。 == 概要 == 本古墳は前方部を南側に向け後円部を北側に向けた墳丘長115[[メートル]]の[[前方後円墳]]である。前方部最大幅53メートル、くびれ幅約47メートル、後円部径60メートルの規模を持ち、墳丘の高さは前方部約10メートル、後円部は約12メートルで、前方部と後円部の比高差は2メートルほどある。墳丘の周りを盾形に巡る三重の周溝を持ち、周溝を含めた規模は全長174メートルで、前方部側最大幅104メートル、後円部側最大幅113メートルを測る。 出土遺物には、頭椎大刀、圭頭大刀、刀身、金銅製刀子鞘片、多数の鉄鏃、金銅製の耳環、水晶製切子玉、ヒスイ製の勾玉、多数のガラス玉・小玉があった。 本古墳で注目すべきは、前室・奥室を設けた複室構造を持ち、奥室の北側北壁に接して[[石棺]]を設けた特異な[[横穴式石室]]である。後円部墳頂から南東側に設けられ開口部を南に向けた埋葬施設の全長は約9メートル、奥室の奥壁には朱が塗られていた（現状は崩落していて確認できない）。大型の古墳にもかかわらず[[埴輪]]を持たず、終末期の前方後円墳とされ、前方部方向が南方向に位置することなども特徴的である。 また三重に巡る周溝は例が少なく、現在確認されているのは本古墳を含め8例三重の周溝を持つ前方後円墳は、本古墳の他に[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[百舌鳥古墳群]]中の[[大仙陵古墳]]([[仁徳天皇]]陵)、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]の東光寺剣塚古墳、同県[[うきは市]]若宮古墳群中の[[月岡古墳]]、同県[[久留米市]]の[[御塚・権現塚古墳|御塚古墳]]、[[群馬県]][[高崎市]][[保渡田古墳群]]中の八幡塚古墳、同県[[藤岡市]][[白石古墳群]]中の[[七輿山古墳]]、[[埼玉県]][[行田市]][[埼玉古墳群]]中の[[鉄砲山古墳]]の7例がある。のみである。他の7例はいずれも[[5世紀]]～[[6世紀]]の大型古墳であるが、本古墳は6世紀末～[[7世紀]]初頭の築造とされ大型の古墳が造られなくなった時期でもあり、この時期のものとしては日本最大である。 File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|前方部の墳丘 File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|後円部の墳丘 File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|墳丘を廻る周溝、左奥は前方部 File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|前方部の頂部から、正面奥は後円部 File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|後円部の頂部から、正面奥は前方部 File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|前方部の前に鎮座する箱根神社 == 周辺 == 山武市には多くの古墳があり[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/institute/human/kiyou/81/13.pdf 2009年度 日本大学人文科学研究所共同研究 研究報告 千葉県における古墳調査と埴輪「文理学部所蔵考古資料の研究」]、[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]沿いにはこの大堤権現塚古墳を含み2基の前方後円墳と円墳7基が確認されている大堤古墳群の他、前方後円墳4基と円墳15基のある[[蕪木古墳群]]や、円墳としては千葉県最大の[[山室姫塚古墳]]など17基の円墳より成る大塚古墳群があり、市町境を越えた[[横芝光町]]中台には表情豊かな人物埴輪を出土したことで知られる[[芝山古墳群]]もある。 [[境川 (山武市)|境川]]東岸には、4世紀後半頃の銅鏡4面が出土したことで知られる島戸境1号墳を有する島戸境古墳群や、豊富な馬具類を出土したカブト塚古墳を有する麻生新田古墳群、多数の埴輪を出土した経僧塚古墳を有する真行寺古墳群、この大堤権現塚古墳に続く最終段階の前方後円墳と考えられる[[胡麻手台16号墳]]を有する胡麻手台古墳群、あるいはその後の[[仏教]][[寺院]]であり、[[武射郡]]を代表する古代寺院と考えられる真行寺廃寺跡などもある。また[[作田川]]東岸には、[[4世紀]]前半頃の重圏紋倭鏡が発見された北野遺跡があり、古墳時代終末期の1辺62メートルの[[方墳]][[駄ノ塚古墳]]や墳丘長約90メートルの前方後円墳西ノ台古墳など、前方後円墳3基、円墳29基、方墳3基が存在した板附古墳群もある。 == 交通アクセス == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[総武本線]][[松尾駅 (千葉県)|松尾駅]]より徒歩15分。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『日本古墳大辞典』 [[東京堂出版]]、1989年。ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * 『千葉県の地名（日本歴史地名大系 12）』[[平凡社]]、1996年。ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html 大堤権現塚古墳] 山武市教育委員会 * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html 武社国の古墳（松尾町・横芝町の古墳）]芝山町立芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつつみこんけんつかこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:山武市の歴史]] [[Category:千葉県指定史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称=大堤権現塚古墳 |所属=大堤古墳群 |画像=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|大堤権現塚古墳]] |所在地=千葉県山武市松尾町大堤482 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|scale:20000}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長174m（周溝を含む） |築造年代=6世紀末～7世紀初頭 |出土品=頭椎大刀、圭頭大刀等 |史跡指定=1990年（平成2年）3月16日・千葉県指定 |埋葬施設=横穴式石室 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=大堤権現塚
古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=前方後円墳-南南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''大堤権現塚古墳'''（おおつつみごんげんづかこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武市]]松尾町大堤にある[[古墳]]。[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|千葉県の史跡]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|title=大堤権現塚古墳|publisher=千葉県|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|accessdate=2020-04-19}}。 == 概要 == 本古墳は前方部を南側に向け後円部を北側に向けた墳丘長115[[メートル]]の[[前方後円墳]]である。前方部最大幅53メートル、くびれ幅約47メートル、後円部径60メートルの規模を持ち、墳丘の高さは前方部約10メートル、後円部は約12メートルで、前方部と後円部の比高差は2メートルほどある。墳丘の周りを盾形に巡る三重の周溝を持ち、周溝を含めた規模は全長174メートルで、前方部側最大幅104メートル、後円部側最大幅113メートルを測る。 出土遺物には、頭椎大刀、圭頭大刀、刀身、金銅製刀子鞘片、多数の鉄鏃、金銅製の耳環、水晶製切子玉、ヒスイ製の勾玉、多数のガラス玉・小玉があった。 本古墳で注目すべきは、前室・奥室を設けた複室構造を持ち、奥室の北側北壁に接して[[石棺]]を設けた特異な[[横穴式石室]]である。後円部墳頂から南東側に設けられ開口部を南に向けた埋葬施設の全長は約9メートル、奥室の奥壁には朱が塗られていた（現状は崩落していて確認できない）。大型の古墳にもかかわらず[[埴輪]]を持たず、終末期の前方後円墳とされ、前方部方向が南方向に位置することなども特徴的である。 また三重に巡る周溝は例が少なく、現在確認されているのは本古墳を含め8例三重の周溝を持つ前方後円墳は、本古墳の他に[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[百舌鳥古墳群]]中の[[大仙陵古墳]]([[仁徳天皇]]陵)、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]の東光寺剣塚古墳、同県[[うきは市]]若宮古墳群中の[[月岡古墳]]、同県[[久留米市]]の[[御塚・権現塚古墳|御塚古墳]]、[[群馬県]][[高崎市]][[保渡田古墳群]]中の八幡塚古墳、同県[[藤岡市]][[白石古墳群]]中の[[七輿山古墳]]、[[埼玉県]][[行田市]][[埼玉古墳群]]中の[[鉄砲山古墳]]の7例がある。のみである。他の7例はいずれも[[5世紀]]～[[6世紀]]の大型古墳であるが、本古墳は6世紀末～[[7世紀]]初頭の築造とされ大型の古墳が造られなくなった時期でもあり、この時期のものとしては日本最大である。 Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|墳丘を廻る周溝、左奥は前方部 File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|前方部の頂部から、正面奥は後円部 File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|後円部の頂部から、正面奥は前方部 File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|前方部の前に鎮座する箱根神社 == 周辺 == 山武市には多くの古墳があり[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/institute/human/kiyou/81/13.pdf 2009年度 日本大学人文科学研究所共同研究 研究報告 千葉県における古墳調査と埴輪「文理学部所蔵考古資料の研究」]、[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]沿いにはこの大堤権現塚古墳を含み2基の前方後円墳と円墳7基が確認されている大堤古墳群の他、前方後円墳4基と円墳15基のある[[蕪木古墳群]]や、円墳としては千葉県最大の[[山室姫塚古墳]]など17基の円墳より成る大塚古墳群があり、市町境を越えた[[横芝光町]]中台には表情豊かな人物埴輪を出土したことで知られる[[芝山古墳群]]もある。 [[境川 (山武市)|境川]]東岸には、4世紀後半頃の銅鏡4面が出土したことで知られる島戸境1号墳を有する島戸境古墳群や、豊富な馬具類を出土したカブト塚古墳を有する麻生新田古墳群、多数の埴輪を出土した経僧塚古墳を有する真行寺古墳群、この大堤権現塚古墳に続く最終段階の前方後円墳と考えられる[[胡麻手台16号墳]]を有する胡麻手台古墳群、あるいはその後の[[仏教]][[寺院]]であり、[[武射郡]]を代表する古代寺院と考えられる真行寺廃寺跡などもある。また[[作田川]]東岸には、[[4世紀]]前半頃の重圏紋倭鏡が発見された北野遺跡があり、古墳時代終末期の1辺62メートルの[[方墳]][[駄ノ塚古墳]]や墳丘長約90メートルの前方後円墳西ノ台古墳など、前方後円墳3基、円墳29基、方墳3基が存在した板附古墳群もある。 == 交通アクセス == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[総武本線]][[松尾駅 (千葉県)|松尾駅]]より徒歩15分。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『日本古墳大辞典』 [[東京堂出版]]、1989年。ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * 『千葉県の地名（日本歴史地名大系 12）』[[平凡社]]、1996年。ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html 大堤権現塚古墳] 山武市教育委員会 * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html 武社国の古墳（松尾町・横芝町の古墳）]芝山町立芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつつみこんけんつかこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:山武市の歴史]] [[Category:千葉県指定史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun | part_of=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=Total length 174 m (including moats) |built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |burial goods=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |Historic Site=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |burial=Horizontal stone chamber |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} * [[Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun | part_of=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=Total length 174 m (including moats) |built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |burial goods=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |Historic Site=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |burial=Horizontal stone chamber |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} * [[Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{citation needed|Ozutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun}} {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun | part_of=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=Total length 174 m (including moats) |built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |burial goods=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |Historic Site=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |burial=Horizontal stone chamber |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Tombs|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkōji Tomb|qq=}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|qq=}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== * [[Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun | part_of=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=Total length 174 m (including moats) |built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |burial goods=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |Historic Site=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |burial=Horizontal stone chamber |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}} in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Tombs|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkōji Tomb|qq=}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|qq=}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== * [[Kaburagi Kofun Group|Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun | part_of=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 04.jpg|280px|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun]] |location=[[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Sanmu City]], Matsuo Town, Ōtsutsumi 482 |coordinates={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=Total length 174 m (including moats) |built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |burial goods=Sword with large pommel, ceremonial swords, and more |Historic Site=Designated by Chiba Prefecture on March 16, 1990 |burial=Horizontal stone chamber |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=56|mark=Keyhole_kofun_south.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|03|N|140|26|56|E}}}} }} '''Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun''' (大堤権現塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Matsuo Town, [[Sanmu City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Chiba Prefecture|historic site of Chiba Prefecture]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-075.html|title=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-04-19}}. == Overview == This kofun is a keyhole-shaped Kofun measuring 115 m in mound length, with the front section oriented to the south and the rear section to the north. The front section measures 53 m at its widest point, with a neck width of approximately 47 m. The rear mound has a diameter of 60 m. The mound height is approximately 10 m for the front section and 12 m for the rear, with a height difference of 2 m between the two. Surrounding the mound are triple concentric moats in a shield type, with a total length of 174 m including the moats. The widest points of the moats measure 104 m on the front side and 113 m on the rear side. burial goods unearthed include swords, gold-plated ornamental fragments, crystal beads, jade magatama, and numerous glass beads. The burial facility is notable for its unique horizontal stone chamber with a double-room structure. The main burial chamber includes a stone coffin and was painted with vermillion (now collapsed). Despite its large size, this kofun lacks [[haniwa]], making it a terminal period keyhole tomb, believed to have been constructed during the late 6th to early 7th century. The triple moats are rare, with only eight examples confirmed nationwide, including this kofunOther examples include {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}} in the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], the {{ill|Tōkōji Tomb|ja|東光寺剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkōji Tomb|qq=}} in [[Fukuoka City]], and others from the late 5th to 6th centuries.. Among terminal period tombs, this is the largest of its kind in Japan. File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 07.jpg|Front section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 03.jpg|Rear section of the mound File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 10.jpg|Moats around the mound, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 08.jpg|View from the top of the front section, rear section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 09.jpg|View from the top of the rear section, front section in the distance File:Otsutsumi Gongenzuka Tomb (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|Hakone Shrine at the front of the mound == Surrounding Area == Sanmu City features numerous kofun clusters, including: * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|ja|大堤古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsutsumi Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, comprising this kofun and others along the [[Kidogawa River]]. * {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, with 4 keyhole-shaped kofun and 15 round kofun. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamuro Himetsuka Kofun|qq=}}, the largest round kofun in Chiba Prefecture. == Access == * [[JR East]] [[Sōbu Main Line]], a 15-minute walk from [[Matsuo Station (Chiba)|Matsuo Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7. * ''Chiba-ken no Chimei'' (Place names of Chiba Prefecture). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1. == See Also == * [[Kofun built]] * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001477.html Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/matuo.html Ancient Kofun in Matsuo and Yokoshiba Towns] - Shibayama Town Kofun and Haniwa Museum {{japanese-history-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} ==See Also== * [[Kaburagi Kofun Group|Kabuki Kofun Cluster]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|</nowiki>蕪木古墳群<nowiki>|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]] * {{nihongo2|[[:ja:大堤権現塚古墳|大堤権現塚古墳]]}}
